title: fix: auto-recover stale heatmap screenshots stuck in processing
body:
## Problem

Users reported "Screenshot generation timed out" when viewing heatmap screenshots. The root cause: when a heatmap is created, a Celery task generates the screenshot. The frontend polls for up to 2 minutes waiting for it to complete. If the task doesn't finish in time (worker crash, queue loss, server restart), the `SavedHeatmap` record stays in `status='processing'` forever, and every subsequent visit triggers the same 2-minute polling timeout.

**Why it affects one user but not
author: jabahamondes
summary: Addresses a critical issue where heatmap screenshots could become permanently stuck in processing, affecting user experience.

title: fix: Move sleep to async part of the code
body:
## Problem

~`time.sleep` may be blocking.~ All resources I've found indicate this is likely not the issue.

However, having too many infinite loops could starve the executor's thread pool. So, we should still consider waiting outside the thread pool so that others can get a thread to run. The downside is more context switching when we retry a lot, but this should be rare to begin with (and we sleep up to 32 seconds, so plenty of time to run other stuff). 

<!-- Who are we building for, wh
author: tomasfarias
summary: Moves sleep functionality to the asynchronous part of the code to prevent blocking the executor's thread pool.

title: fix(llma): inject flag-gated columns into persisted generations column sets
body:
## Problem

The generations table `generationsColumns` reducer uses `{ persist: true }`, saving the column selection to localStorage. When a user visits the page before a feature flag (sentiment, tools) is enabled for them, their column config gets persisted without the flag-gated column. Once the flag is later enabled, the persisted columns always take priority over the flag-based defaults, so the new column never appears.

This affects any user who visited the generations page before the `llm-
author: andrewm4894
summary: Ensures flag-gated columns are correctly injected into persisted generations column sets.

title: chore: remove pyright-lsp plugin from project settings
body:
Removes the pyright-lsp plugin from the shared project `.claude/settings.json`.

**Why:** On a monorepo this size, pyright LSP consumes significant context tokens — diagnostics get injected on every file operation, and reference lookups on common symbols can return hundreds of results. There are also known upstream issues: false-positive hint-level diagnostics eating context ([anthropics/claude-code#26634](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/26634)) and a memory leak with diagnostic
author: webjunkie
summary: Removed the pyright-lsp plugin from project settings to improve performance in the monorepo.

title: feat(flags): add negative cache for invalid API tokens
body:
## Summary

- Remove the `posthog_tombstone_total{operation="hypercache_miss"}` metric from the hypercache that incorrectly fired for every invalid token lookup (~1% of traffic). The existing `result=missing` counter already tracks this case.
- Add an in-memory negative cache (Moka-based, bounded, TTL) to the feature-flags service that prevents repeated Redis/S3/PG lookups for API tokens that don't correspond to any team.

### Why

Grafana metrics showed that the tombstone fires at the same rate
author: haacked
summary: Introduces a negative cache for invalid API tokens to improve performance in the feature flags service.

title: fix(hogql): add missing 'next_join' and 'alias' fields in JSON representation of JoinExpr
body:
## Problem

Table function expressions used as the left-hand side of a `JOIN` (e.g. `SELECT * FROM numbers(10) JOIN events ON 1`) caused a crash in the C++ parser with `ParsingError: JoinExpr is missing 'next_join' field`. `TableFunctionExpr` constructed a `JoinExpr` node without setting `next_join`, which `chainJoinExprs` requires on every node it traverses.

## Changes

Set `next_join = null` and `alias = null` on the `JoinExpr` produced by `TableFunctionExpr` in `parser_json.cpp`, match
author: georgemunyoro
summary: Fixes a critical parsing error in the C++ parser that caused crashes during JOIN operations.

The logs viewer grouped logs query has an inner subquery which uses LOG_VIEWER_LIMIT = 100, but the outer SELECT has no LIMIT. The backend then applies the 100-row default return limit, so the request returns 100 rows total, even though the intention was to fetch 100 _groups_

This leads to incredibly confusing groups in the log viewer due to the earliest logs of each group being truncated off of the results (on both realtime destinations and workflows):

First group in the res
author: havenbarnes
summary: Fixes a limitation in the grouped log viewer that caused confusion by truncating the earliest logs of each group.

title: fix(replay): make generateKey idempotent to prevent encryption key overwrites
body:
## Summary

- Add `ConditionExpression: 'attribute_not_exists(session_id)'` to the DynamoDB `PutItem` in `generateKey` so a key is only written if one does not already exist
- On `ConditionalCheckFailedException`, fall back to `getKey` to return the existing key

## Problem

`DynamoDBKeyStore.generateKey()` used an unconditional `PutItem`. If blobby called `generateKey` twice for the same session, the second call would silently overwrite the first key in DynamoDB. All S3 blocks encrypted with th
author: TueHaulund
summary: Made the key generation process idempotent to prevent overwriting encryption keys in DynamoDB.

title: fix(llm-gateway): use shorter cache TTL for OAuth tokens to handle token refresh
body:
## Problem

OAuth tokens in the LLM gateway were cached for 15 minutes (`auth_cache_ttl = 900`). When a token refresh occurred, the old access token was deleted from the database, so the main Django API correctly returned 401. However, the LLM gateway's in-memory cache still held the old token as valid — the only expiration check on cache hits was against `token_expires_at` (the token's original lifetime), not whether it had been revoked/deleted by a refresh.

This meant revoked OAuth tokens cou
author: skoob13
summary: Fixed a caching issue in the LLM gateway that could lead to using revoked OAuth tokens.

title: chore(flags): add cross-language property operator consistency checks
body:
## Problem

Property filter operators are defined in TypeScript/Python and independently re-implemented in Rust (feature flag evaluator) and HogVM (workflows/CDP). When a new operator is added to one evaluation path but not another, things break at runtime with no warning. This happened twice in one week:

1. **Semver operators** worked in HogQL but the HogVM bytecode referenced `sortableSemver` which didn't exist in the STL, [breaking workflows](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C07QD3LT8U
author: dmarticus
summary: Added consistency checks for property operators across different languages to prevent runtime errors.

title: fix(experiments): hardcode experiment type to 'product' in MCP
body:
## Problem

The MCP `experiment-create` tool exposed a `type` field with `'product'` and `'web'` options. LLMs frequently chose `'web'` because many experiments run on the web, but `'web'` actually means **no-code experiments** which the MCP should never create. The naming is misleading — `'web'` should really be called `'no-code'` and `'product'` should be `'code'`.

We should also improve the API layer. But for now, fix the MCP so the user experience is improved.

## Changes

- Removed
author: andehen
summary: Corrects the experiment type selection in the MCP to prevent misleading options for users.

title: fix(signals): Store full segments in S3 instead of re-querying ClickHouse
body:
## Problem

After fetching segments from ClickHouse in a2, we were storing only `document_ids` in S3. Then a3 and a4 had to re-query CH with `WHERE document_id IN (...)` to get the same metadata back - wasteful and doesn't scale (queries become too large with the IDs embedded).

## Changes

Store full `VideoSegment` objects in S3 alongside `distinct_ids`. Now a3 and a4 load everything from S3.

Removed a bunch of duplicated metadata parsing too.

## How did you test this code?

Exist
author: Twixes
summary: Improves efficiency by storing full segments in S3, eliminating the need for redundant queries to ClickHouse.

title: feat(analytics-platform): Allow dots in PostHog client identifier parsing
body:
## Problem

The regex pattern used to extract the PostHog client identifier from the User-Agent header only matches word characters and hyphens (`[\w-]+`). This prevents client identifiers containing dots (e.g., "posthog/hog.dev") from being properly recognized and forwarded in API requests for source attribution.

## Changes

Updated the regex pattern in the User-Agent parsing logic from `/posthog\/([\w-]+)/` to `/posthog\/([\w.-]+)/` to include dots in the allowed character set for clien
author: robbie-c
summary: Updated the PostHog client identifier parsing to allow dots, enhancing source attribution capabilities.

title: fix(experiments): await refresh loaders before completion event
body:
## Problem
The `experiment results refresh completed` telemetry event is emitted before metric loaders finish.
This causes `total_duration_ms` to be unrealistically low and both `successful_count`/`errored_count` to be zero in real refreshes, even when metric queries finish seconds later.

## Changes
- Switched `refreshExperimentResults` to await `asyncActions.loadPrimaryMetricsResults`, `asyncActions.loadSecondaryMetricsResults`, and `asyncActions.loadExposures` instead of awaiting dispatch-onl
author: andehen
summary: Ensures that the experiment results telemetry event reflects accurate metrics by awaiting the completion of metric loaders.

title: feat(llma): cache sentiment results in Temporal activity
body:
## Problem

When a user opens the traces table, the frontend fires off sentiment classification requests. If the user navigates away (clicks into a trace, switches tabs) before the workflow completes, the HTTP request is dropped and the results are lost -- even though the Temporal workflow runs to completion on the worker. When the user navigates back, classification starts from scratch, wasting the work that was already done.

## Changes

- Move cache writes from the Django API view into the Te

summary: Caches sentiment results to prevent loss of data when users navigate away from the traces table.

title: fix(llma): bump sentiment timeout from 60s to 120s
body:
## Problem

The sentiment classification activity timeout (60s) is too tight. Grafana dashboard shows:

- Activity execution latency P95 at 59.1s (right at the limit)
- Activity execution latency P99 at 1.78 min (well over the limit)
- 24h workflow success rate at 99.858% — the ~0.14% failures are timeout kills

Tail activities processing larger traces legitimately need more time for ONNX inference but are being killed at 60s.

## Changes

Bump both `ACTIVITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` and `WORKFLOW_TIMEO
author: andrewm4894
summary: Increased the sentiment classification activity timeout to prevent legitimate processing delays from being killed.

## Problem

The flag definitions cache (`flags_with_cohorts.json` / `flags_without_cohorts.json`) used for SDK local evaluation has no operational tooling for manual recovery. Unlike the other caches (`flags.json`, `team_metadata`), operators have no way to:

1. **Manually warm the cache** — if the cache is cold (after a Redis flush, schema migration, or deployment), there's no command to populate it. Recovery requires waiting for Django signals or SDK requests to hit the database.
2. **Manually
author: haacked

title: feat(flags): add flag definitions cache verification and maintenance tasks
body:
## Problem

The flag definitions HyperCache (used by SDKs for local evaluation) has verification logic and management commands, but no automated scheduled tasks to run them. If a cache entry becomes stale, mismatched, or expires, there's no automated mechanism to detect and fix it — leading to SDKs serving outdated flag definitions until the next signal-triggered update.

## Changes

Adds the same three scheduled Celery tasks that already exist for the flags cache and team metadata cache, now fo
author: haacked
summary: Introduces automated scheduled tasks for verifying and maintaining the flag definitions cache to prevent outdated data in SDKs.

title: feat(flags): add flag definitions cache verification logic
body:
## Problem

The flag definitions cache (`flags_with_cohorts.json` / `flags_without_cohorts.json`) used for SDK local evaluation has no verification mechanism. Unlike the flags cache (`flags.json`) and team metadata cache — which have `verify_team_flags()` and `verify_team_metadata()` — there's no way to detect when cached flag definitions drift from the database. If a Django signal fails or a cache entry expires without a flag change, the stale or missing data goes undetected until an SDK reques
author: haacked
summary: Adds verification logic for the flag definitions cache to detect discrepancies between cached data and the database.

title: feat(personhog-router): add retries
body:
## Problem

The personhog-router makes single-shot gRPC calls to personhog-replica with no retry logic.
Any transient failure (connection blip, backend restart, brief overload) propagates directly to the caller.
As we migrate feature-flags to use personhog-router instead of direct SQL queries,
the router needs to absorb transient backend failures transparently — the same way
feature-flags currently has exponential backoff on its SQL path via `tokio-retry`.


## Changes

**Retry helper
author: nickbest-ph
summary: Implemented retry logic in the personhog-router to handle transient backend failures.

title: fix(replay): prevent infinite recursion in updateAnimation
body:
## Summary

- Replaces the `shouldSeek` parameter on `setCurrentSegment` with a reentrancy guard on `updateAnimation`
- The synchronous kea dispatch chain `updateAnimation → setCurrentSegment → seekToTimestamp → updateAnimation` caused a stack overflow during segment transitions, particularly with skip inactivity enabled
- The previous fix (`shouldSeek=false`) prevented the recursion but broke playback — same-window segment transitions stopped the animation loop because no next frame was schedul
author: TueHaulund
summary: Fixes an infinite recursion issue in the animation update process, ensuring smooth playback during segment transitions.

title: fix(surveys): prevent targeting flags from reactivating when archiving a stopped survey
body:
## Problem

The survey update serializer used `validated_data.get("end_date")` to decide whether targeting flags should be active. This returns `None` for any PATCH that doesn't include `end_date`, which caused two bugs:

1. **Archiving a stopped survey reactivated its flags** — the archive PATCH doesn't include `end_date` (it's already set), so the serializer treated it as "no end date" and set flags back to `active=True`
2. **Any unrelated PATCH to a stopped survey reactivated its flags** — e.
author: haacked
summary: Fixes a bug that caused targeting flags to reactivate when archiving or updating stopped surveys.

title: fix(flags): canonical log lines silenty no-op in parallle path
body:
## Problem

Canonical log counters (`cohorts_evaluated`, `property_cache_hits/misses`, `flags_device_id_bucketing`, `person_properties_not_cached`, `group_properties_not_cached`) are silently lost when flag evaluation runs on the rayon thread pool.

`CANONICAL_LOG` is stored in a `tokio::task_local!`, which is only accessible from the originating tokio task. Rayon worker threads have no access to it, so all `with_canonical_log()` calls during parallel evaluation are silent no-ops. This means
author: matheus-vb
summary: Fixes an issue where canonical log counters are lost during parallel flag evaluations.

title: feat: change personhog upsert API to accept distinct_ids and resolve …
body:
## Problem

The personhog upsert API (`UpsertHashKeyOverridesRequest`) required callers to pre-resolve distinct IDs to person IDs before sending the request. This meant the feature-flags service had to call `get_person_by_distinct_id` for the first distinct ID, then build `HashKeyOverrideInput { person_id, feature_flag_key }` entries to send.

The SQL path resolves **all** distinct IDs (typically `[identified_user, anon_hash_key]`) and writes overrides for every person found. But the personh
author: nickbest-ph
summary: Enhances the personhog upsert API to accept distinct IDs directly, simplifying the request process.

title: fix(feature-flags): skip dependency extraction for inactive feature flags

## Problem

The `posthog_tombstone_total{operation="flag_dependency_missing"}` metric fires at ~800-1000/sec in prod-us. Investigation revealed this comes from a small number of inactive or orphaned survey-targeting flags that reference deleted feature flags. When the dependency graph is built, these stale references trigger `MissingDependency` errors and tombstone emissions on every request for those teams.

The root issue: `from_nodes()` extracts dependencies for ALL flags including inactive o
author: haacked

Adds `/v1/messages/count_tokens` and `/{product}/v1/messages/count_tokens` proxy routes to the LLM gateway. Routes forward directly to Anthropic's count_tokens API via httpx, bypassing litellm/callbacks so no `$ai_generation` events are created.

## Problem

Twig traces in PostHog project 2 are spammed with thousands of identical `$ai_generation` events that are noise, waste money, and degrade LLM Analytics observability.

### Impact (measured 2026-02-25, last 7 days)

| Metric | Val

One misconfigured goal breaks all three query runners because they share the unified conversion goals CTE via UNION ALL.

author: jabahamondes

title: fix(llma): prefer $ai_trace event distinct_id in trace queries
body:
## Problem

LLM analytics traces were showing "unknown" for the person when the earliest event in a trace (e.g. `$ai_span` or `$ai_generation`) had a different `distinct_id` than the `$ai_trace` event. This happens in server-side SDK scenarios where internal span/generation events use a server-side ID but the `$ai_trace` event carries the actual user's `distinct_id`.

The root cause was that both `trace_query_runner.py` and `traces_query_runner.py` used `argMin(distinct_id, timestamp)` to pi
author: christiaan-ph
summary: Fixes LLM analytics traces to correctly display the user's distinct_id in trace queries.

title: chore(flags): allow override when determining cpu core count
body:
## Problem

* `std::thread::available_parallelism()` reads the cgroup CFS quota, which maps to the Kubernetes CPU **limit**, not the CPU request. In prod-us we set `requests=8000m` and will set `limits=12000m` (the extra headroom avoids CFS throttling during Rayon bursts), so `available_parallelism()` would return 12 instead of 8. This would oversizes both thread pools:

| For US env          | Current (12 detected) | Intended (8 cores) |
| ----------------- | --------------------- | ------
author: matheus-vb
summary: Updated CPU core count determination to prevent oversizing thread pools in production.

- Lowered `MIN_CLUSTER_SIZE_FRACTION_MIN` from 0.05 to 0.02 so the new default is within the valid range
- Updated the function signature default in `clustering.py`
- Updated the frontend defau

The sentiment lazy loader debounces visible trace IDs into chunks of 5, then fires API calls for each chunk. Previously these were processed sequentially in a `for` loop with `await`, meaning each 8-
author: andrewm4894

## Problem

We should improve our data on perceived performance. The existing events (`experiment metric finished`, `experiment metric timeout`, etc.) lack timing data, caching info, metric context, and error detail. The generic `performQuery` tracks `duration` and `is_cached`, but these aren't correlated to experiment-specific context (experiment ID, metric index, primary/secondary, etc.).

Key gaps:
- No `duration_ms` on `experiment metric finished` — can't measure per-metric perceived lo
author: andehen
summary: Enhances experiment metric tracking by adding client-side real user monitoring for better performance insights.

## Problem

The `.only()` clause in `update_all_orgs_billing_quotas()` is missing `customer_trust_scores` and `customer_id`. When Django accesses these deferred fields, it silently issues a separate SELECT per org - a hidden N+1 problem.

- `customer_trust_scores` is accessed for every org (~251K deferred SELECTs per run)
- `customer_id` is accessed for every limited/suspended org (~2.3K deferred SELECTs per run)

This is a follow up from #48874 

## Changes

Add `organization__custom

## Problem

Sentiment classification activities take ~17s per workflow when batching 10 trace IDs.
With `MAX_CONCURRENT_ACTIVITIES=1` per pod, this means users wait ~17s for results
to appear in the traces table after scrolling.

## Changes

- `BATCH_MAX_TRACE_IDS`: 10 -> 5

Halves the work per workflow so activities finish in ~8s instead of ~17s, returning
results to the UI faster. With 12 pods available (charts PR #8552), doubling the
number of workflows has no capacity impact -- Temporal dist

title: fix(llma): reduce sentiment query workload with per-trace window function
body:
## Problem

Sentiment classification workers are saturating CPU and timing out on large traces (e.g. multi-turn AI conversations with 500-1000 generations). The ClickHouse query fetches up to 500 rows with a global LIMIT, so one monster trace can eat the entire budget and transfer 100MB+ of JSON to the worker.

## Changes

Push filtering into the ClickHouse query to bound data transfer and worker load:

- **Per-trace window function**: `row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY trace_id ORDER BY timestamp
author: andrewm4894

The `generate_demo_data` command creates EventDefinitions by inferring them from ClickHouse data via `infer_taxonomy_for_team()`. If the simulated data doesn't happen to include common events like `$pageview`, `$pageleave`, `$autocapture`, or `$screen`, their EventDefinition records won't be created. author: MattBro

title: fix(flags): ensure zrem expiry cleanup always runs in HyperCache.clear_cache

## Problem

Expiry tracking cleanup (`zrem` from Redis sorted set) was duplicated in `clear_flags_cache` and `clear_team_metadata_cache`. If cache deletion threw an exception, the `zrem` was skipped, leaving stale entries in the expiry sorted set.

## Changes

- Moves `zrem` logic into a new `_remove_expiry_tracking` method on `HyperCache`, called from `clear_cache` inside a `finally` block so it always runs
- Handles Team objects, int IDs, and string tokens; mismatched key types are silently sk
author: haacked
summary: Fixes a cache cleanup issue in HyperCache to ensure stale entries are properly removed, preventing potential data inconsistencies.

title: fix(toolbar): dropdown menu styling
body:
## Problem

The toolbar's dropdown menu (MoreMenu) renders completely unstyled — giant icons, no spacing, no colors. This happens because `FloatingPortal` falls back to `document.body` (outside the shadow DOM) due to a timing issue in `useFloatingContainer`, so the shadow-scoped CSS never applies.

Additionally, toolbar modals (e.g. screenshot upload) show black text on a black background in dark mode because `:host { all: initial }` resets `color` to black and nothing re-sets it to a theme-awar
author: fercgomes
summary: Fixes styling issues in the toolbar's dropdown menu and modals, improving user experience in dark mode.

title: chore(dags): fail cleanup step on s3 delete errors and log object count

## Summary

- Check the `Errors` key in the response and raise `dagster.Failure` (which marks the op as failed in the Dagster UI) when any object deletion fails
- Count actually deleted objects and include the count in the log, making it easy to spot cases where no objects were found (e.g. prefix mismatch)

## Test plan

- [ ]

title: fix(ci): pass app token to checkout so OpenAPI push triggers CI
body:
Follow-up to #48711 based on reviewer feedback.

The previous fix (unset extraheader) worked, but the proper pattern is to pass the app token directly to `actions/checkout` so it sets the `http.extraheader` to the app token from the start — same as what snapshot commit jobs already do.

Move the "Get app token" step to before "Checkout this PR" and pass `token: ${{ steps.openapi-app-token.outputs.token || github.token }}`. This way the checkout configures git with the app token's `Authorization`
author: webjunkie
summary: Fixes CI configuration to ensure the app token is correctly passed during the checkout process.

body:
First download our (team-2's) signals with posthog-cli. You'll need to run `posthog-cli login` first, and configure the scopes to also include `query:read`. Then, you can run the query:
```bash
posthog-cli exp query run "select product, document_type, document_id, timestamp, inserted_at, content, metadata from document_embeddings where model_name = 'text-embedding-3-small-1536' and product = 'signals' limit 1000" > signals.json
```

Then run:
```bash
DEBUG=1 python manage.py ingest_signal

title: fix: failing capture build in macOS due to aws-lc-sys
body:
## Problem

After the Cargo.lock refresh in #48562, `aws-lc-sys` was bumped from `0.29.0` to `0.37.1`, crossing the `0.32.0` threshold where jitterentropy C code was added. This code includes macOS SDK headers that reference an undefined `uuid_string_t` type, breaking the Rust build on macOS. Linux is unaffected.

## Changes

- Set `AWS_LC_SYS_NO_JITTER_ENTROPY=1` in `rust/.cargo/config.toml` to skip building the jitterentropy code, which is the upstream recommended workaround
  - https:/
author: jose-sequeira

```python
instance = model.objects.get(id=scheduled_c

title: fix(mcp): use npx for tsx in cloudflare build command
body:
## Problem

The MCP Cloudflare Worker build is broken since #48770 was merged. The build command `tsx scripts/copy-instructions.ts` fails because Cloudflare's build environment doesn't have `node_modules/.bin` in PATH, so the `tsx` binary isn't found:

```
/bin/sh: 1: tsx: not found
```

This means the MCP server hasn't been deploying since that PR merged.

## Changes

Changed `tsx` to `npx tsx` in the wrangler build command so it resolves correctly in Cloudflare's build environment.

## How did
author: MattBro
summary: Resolved a build issue in the MCP Cloudflare Worker by changing the command to use 'npx' for compatibility with the build environment.

title: fix(hobby): fix persistent preview not updating docker image on new commits
body:
**Root cause:** `copyFileWithEnvSubst` was baking `POSTHOG_APP_TAG` (and `REGISTRY_URL`, `POSTHOG_NODE_TAG`) directly into `docker-compose.yml` at install time. `update_existing_deployment` in `hobby-ci.py` correctly updated `.env` with the new SHA, but since `$POSTHOG_APP_TAG` no longer existed in the compose file after install, `docker-compose pull` just kept pulling the old hardcoded image. The deployment appeared to succeed but was running stale code.

A secondary bug enabled this: `UpdateEn

Ordered funnels compute exposures inline on every query, also for the teams that have precomputed exposures cached:

```sql
-- Ordered funnels (before)
entity_metrics AS (
    SELECT
        argMinIf($feature_flag, timestamp, step_0 = 1) as variant,  -- Scan events
        argMinIf(uuid, timestamp, step_0 = 1) AS exposure_event_uuid,
        ...
    FROM metric_events
    GROUP BY entity_id
)
```

Unordered funnels already have a separate exposures CTE that reads from

The `sortableSemver` function existed in HogQL (for ClickHouse queries) but was missing from the Hog bytecode STL, causing failures when used in CDP filters or Hog programs.

## Changes

- Add `extractRegex` to native STL (Python + TypeScript) for regex extraction matching ClickHouse's `extract(haystack, pattern)` behavior
- Create `sortableSemver.hog` using `extractRegex` to parse version strings into comparable integer arrays
- Generate bytecode for both Python and TypeScri

title: fix(llma): fix traces pagination
body:
## Problem
AI traces pagination was producing overlapping results when traces contained multiple events. The subquery was ordering trace IDs by `max(timestamp) DESC` while the main query ordered by `min(timestamp) DESC` (first_timestamp), causing trace positions to shift between pages and breaking offset-based pagination.

## Changes
- Changed the subquery ordering from `max(toTimeZone(events.timestamp, 'UTC')) DESC` to `min(toTimeZone(events.timestamp, 'UTC')) DESC` to match the main query's `O
author: kappa90
summary: This PR fixes overlapping results in AI traces pagination, ensuring accurate data retrieval.

title: fix(llma): render bracket-prefixed text as plain text instead of empty JSON
body:
## Problem

When assistant output starts with `[` (e.g. `[Thinking: The user wants to build a todo app.]I'll build...`), the conversation view displays an empty `[]` instead of the actual content.

This happens because `renderMessageContent` sees `[` and tries `parsePartialJSON`, which treats it as a JSON array. Since `Thinking:` isn't a valid JSON element, the partial parser returns `[]` (empty array), which gets rendered by the JSON viewer.

## Changes

- After `parsePartialJSON`, check if the
author: andrewm4894

title: fix(ci): unset GITHUB_TOKEN extraheader before OpenAPI type push
body:
**Problem:** auto-updating OpenAPI types broke merging — the commit landed but required checks never started.

**Root cause:** `actions/checkout` (in `check-migrations`) runs without a `token:` param, so git gets configured with a `GITHUB_TOKEN`-based `http.https://github.com/.extraheader`. That extraheader takes precedence over URL-embedded credentials, so the later `git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${APP_TOKEN}@..."` has no effect — the push still uses `GITHUB_TOKEN`. GitHub b
author: webjunkie

The trends alert validation code was creating a `ValueError` exception but not raising it, causing the error to be silently ignored instead of properly handling unsupported alert configuration types.

This results in about 8% of the failed alert checks ([insight](https://us.posthog.com/project/2/insights/US61ntVv?dashboard=636477&variables_override={}&filters_override={}&tile_filters_override={})): 

## ![CleanShot 2026-02-23 at 13.00.35.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments

summary: Ensures that unsupported alert configuration types raise an error instead of being silently ignored.

title: fix: Change BridgePage overflow from scroll to auto
body:
## Problem

Fixes a visual bug (and my OCD itch) where a white line appeared at the bottom of the login page on macOS, visible where the browser window corners are rounded. The `overflow: scroll` property forces the browser to always render a scroll container, which created a rendering seam that exposed the body's white background behind the BridgePage.

## Changes

Changing to `overflow: auto` preserves scrolling when content overflows the viewport but avoids the artifact when it doesn't.
author: charlesvien
summary: Fixes a visual bug on the login page by changing the overflow property to prevent a rendering seam.

author: haacked

title: feat(vercel): redirect SSO login to EU when resource not found in US
body:
## Problem

When a Vercel Marketplace EU user clicks SSO login, Vercel sends them to `us.posthog.com/login/vercel/` with a single-use authorization code. The US region doesn't have the EU resource, so SSO fails. We can't proxy the request server-side because the SSO code is consumed on first use — it must be a browser redirect so the code reaches the correct region.

## Changes

- Added `VercelRegionProxyMixin` to `VercelSSOViewSet` for region detection (`current_region`, `is_dev_env`, `EU_DOMAI
author: MattBro
summary: Redirects SSO login for Vercel users to the correct EU region to prevent login failures.

title: feat(llma): add team_id to Temporal error tracking for LLM analytics workflows
body:
## Problem

When Temporal activities fail, the `PostHogClientInterceptor` captures exceptions with properties from `properties_to_log` — but only when the activity takes a single dataclass arg with that property. Most LLM analytics activities take multiple plain args, so their errors are captured without `team_id`, making them impossible to trace back to a team.

## Changes

- Wrap `execute_llm_judge_activity`, `emit_evaluation_event_activity`, and `emit_internal_telemetry_activity` args i
author: Radu-Raicea
summary: Enhanced error tracking for LLM analytics workflows by adding team_id to improve traceability.

title: fix: return empty 204 from Vercel proxy instead of body with {}
body:
## Problem

EU Vercel installations were failing with "An error occurred while creating the Account" despite our API returning 204 success responses.

The proxy in `VercelRegionProxyMixin` was wrapping 204 No Content responses from the EU region with `Response(data={}, status=204)`. Since `{}` is truthy in Python, `dispatch` then serialized it into `HttpResponse(content="{}", status=204, content_type="application/json")` — violating the HTTP spec (204 must have no body) and causing Vercel to rej
author: MattBro
summary: Fixed the Vercel proxy to return a proper 204 No Content response, resolving installation issues for EU users.

title: fix: apply searchTerm filter in logs query runner
body:
## Problem

The `searchTerm` parameter in `LogsQuery` was accepted by the schema and passed through the API, but **never actually used** when building the SQL WHERE clause in `LogsQueryRunnerMixin.where()`. This meant any free-text search on logs (including via the MCP `logs-query` tool) was silently ignored and returned unfiltered results.

## Changes

- Added `body ILIKE '%<searchTerm>%'` filter to the `where()` method in `LogsQueryRunnerMixin` when `searchTerm` is set
- Added unit tests verif
author: MattBro
summary: Fixed a critical issue where the searchTerm filter was not applied in logs queries, leading to unfiltered results.

title: feat(flags): tokio runtime monitoring
body:
## Problem

We currently have no visibility into how the Tokio runtime behaves under production load in the feature flags service.
When we see elevated p99 latencies or timeouts, we can't distinguish between:
- The runtime being saturated (too few worker threads for the workload)
- The runtime being oversized (wasting memory on idle threads)
- Load imbalance across workers (one thread bottlenecked while others idle)
- Blocking work starving the async thread pool (`spawn_blocking` contenti
author: matheus-vb
summary: Implemented monitoring for the Tokio runtime in the feature flags service to improve performance visibility.

title: fix: capture Vercel API errors in PostHog error tracking
body:
## Problem

Vercel integration errors on EU were completely invisible — no alerts, no error tracking, nothing in PostHog. When `VERCEL_CLIENT_INTEGRATION_SECRET` was missing from EU's AWS Secrets Manager, users got silent failures with no way for us to know.

Three root causes:
1. `VercelErrorResponseMixin` bypassed the global `drf-exceptions-hog` exception handler by calling bare DRF `exception_handler()` directly, so non-DRF exceptions never hit `capture_exception`
2. `authenticate_sso`'s catc
author: MattBro
summary: This PR addresses the lack of error tracking for Vercel integration errors, ensuring that such issues are captured and alerted in PostHog.

When adding logs to backfills, I overrode the `workflow_id` key with the value for the currently running backfill run (what a mouthful). This makes filtering logs a bit awkward, as one must look for a different global filter than just the backfill workflow id. 

So let's use a different key.

summary: This PR resolves an issue with log filtering by using a different key for backfill logs, improving the clarity of log management.

title: chore(ci): update uv version check to enforce workflow pins
body:
The script previously flagged workflow `version:` pins as errors, based on the assumption that `setup-uv` reads `required-version` from `pyproject.toml` automatically. That's true, but when the constraint is a range (`~=0.10.2`), `setup-uv` still queries the GitHub API to resolve it — which burns through the shared installation token rate limit under concurrent load.

Check 2 is flipped: instead of "no pins allowed", it now verifies every `setup-uv` usage has an exact pin matching `pyproject.tom
author: webjunkie
summary: Updates the CI script to enforce version pinning for better compatibility checks.

title: fix(ci): pin setup-uv to 0.10.2 across all workflows
body:
Unpinned `setup-uv` makes a GitHub API call on every job to resolve the latest release version. With 60 backend shards + 16 other workflows all firing concurrently, this burns through the shared installation token budget (5000 req/hr) and causes rate limit failures in unrelated jobs (e.g. `dorny/paths-filter` failing with "API rate limit exceeded for installation").

Root cause: #48557 removed the `version:` pins to bump from 0.9.9 → 0.10.2 but left them unpinned instead of re-pinning to the new
author: webjunkie
summary: Pinned setup-uv version to prevent GitHub API rate limit failures in CI workflows.

title: Fix survey stats loading order with archived response UUIDs
body:
## Problem

The survey stats were being loaded before archived response UUIDs were fetched, which meant the `archivedResponsesFilter` wasn't populated when stats queries ran. This could cause incomplete or incorrect stats data.

## Changes

Reordered the survey initialization flow to ensure archived response UUIDs are loaded first:

- Moved `loadArchivedResponseUuids()` call to the initial survey load handler
- Created a new `loadArchivedResponseUuidsSuccess` listener that triggers stats loading

- Switch debug logging from truncated `stringBody` to the reliable `request.body` field (matching the incoming webhook template pattern)

body:
## Problem

[Thread](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C0ACRAMJUAG/p1771518612503749)

- `POST /api/organizations/` requires `TimeSensitiveActionPermission`, which returns 403 if the session is stale (>2 hours)
- `createOrganization()` was missing the `checkReauthentication()` call, so the request was sent without re-auth
- `updateOrganization()` already had this check — `createOrganization()` was simply missed

## Changes

- Added `checkReauthentication()` to `createOrganization()` i

body:
## Problem

For HogQL expressions, we currently don't support well aggregation functions that operate on non-numeric input (such as `uniqExact`, etc). We currently cast all values to floats, that isn't always the correct thing to do.

## Changes
- Skip `toFloat` wrapping for HogQL aggregation functions that don't need numeric input (`uniqExact`, `count`, etc ), preserving raw string/non-numeric values

title: fix(data-modeling): Fix column case sensitivity failure in materialize_view_activity after deltalake 1.4.0 upgrade
body:
## Problem

After the deltalake upgrade to 1.4.0 (#45956), materialized view jobs that produce more than one batch (>100MB) fail when the HogQL query has mixed-case column names (e.g., `Event`, `DistinctId`).

The root cause: `schema_mode="merge"` in `write_deltalake()` triggers deltalake 1.4.0's new DataFusion logical plan path, which lowercases identifiers via `enable_ident_normalization`. The schema reconciliation  then does a case-sensitive comparison between the lowercased identifiers a
author: estefaniarabadan
summary: Fixes a critical issue with materialized view jobs failing due to case sensitivity after a recent upgrade.

author: jose-sequeira

title: fix: Change invalid rrweb events handling from DLQ to DROP with warning
body:
## Problem

Messages with no valid rrweb events should be dropped with an ingestion warning rather than sent to the Dead Letter Queue (DLQ). This provides better visibility into data quality issues while avoiding unnecessary DLQ processing.

## Changes

- Modified `parse-message-step.ts` to return a `DROP` result with an ingestion warning instead of a `DLQ` result when no valid rrweb events are found in a message
- The warning includes the reason `message_contained_no_valid_rrweb_events` with em
author: jose-sequeira
summary: Improves data quality handling by dropping invalid rrweb events with a warning instead of sending them to the Dead Letter Queue.

title: chore(deps): bump uv from 0.9.9 to 0.10.2
body:
**uv** 0.9.9 → 0.10.2 across flox, Dockerfiles, and CI workflows.

The `version:` pin is removed from all 12 workflow files — `setup-uv` now reads the exact pin from `pyproject.toml` (`required-version = "==0.10.2"`), so future bumps only touch pyproject.toml + 3 Dockerfiles.

Also drops the `duckdb` import check from `bin/start` since `uv sync` runs automatically in several places.

**0.10.0 breaking changes** (checked, none affect us):
- Removed Bookworm/Alpine 3.21/Python 3.8 Docker images —

summary: Updated dependencies for improved CI workflows and Dockerfiles without affecting user-facing features.

title: fix(llma): stabilize trace loading and UI follow-up fixes
body:
## Problem

LLM analytics pages have several frontend reliability issues that surface as user-facing bugs and browser console errors:
- Trace pages can fail to load/select events reliably.
- Generation-to-trace navigation can open without enough context to select the intended event.
- Some AI/person data loading paths are unstable and can error when team context is unavailable.
- Shared UI primitives trigger avoidable console warnings/errors (`findDOMNode`, ref handling in wrapped actions).

##
author: richardsolomou
summary: Resolved multiple reliability issues in LLM analytics pages that were causing user-facing bugs and errors.

title: fix(session-recording): fix pagination bug in encryption migration command
body:
## Summary
- The `migrate_session_recording_encryption` management command used Django's `Paginator` (OFFSET-based) while mutating the queryset via `.exclude(session_recording_encryption=True)`. Each batch update shifted the queryset, causing every other batch to be skipped — updating exactly half the intended teams.
- Fix by materializing target IDs upfront and batching over the list. Also switches from `bulk_update` (loads objects) to `QuerySet.update()` (single SQL UPDATE per batch).

## Test
author: TueHaulund
summary: Fixed a pagination bug in the session recording encryption migration command that was causing incomplete updates.

title: fix(notebooks): clear local content on conflict so reload shows latest version

## Problem

When two users edit the same notebook concurrently, the conflict warning appears correctly but clicking "Reload to see the latest content" does **not** show the other user's version.

[LOOM](https://www.loom.com/share/4d96d96d9999480a81d9572851032c51)

## Changes

Clear `localContent` when a 409 conflict is caught so that after reload the editor falls through to the server content.

## How did you test this code?

Manually
author: yasen-posthog
summary: Fixes a conflict issue in the notebook editor that prevents users from seeing the latest version when reloading.

title: fix: increase quota limiting timeout
body:
### Problem

The `run-quota-limiting` workflow has been failing in US for ~24h with activity timeouts. EU completes in ~30min, but US now exceeds 60min, probably due to more data across all the sequential ClickHouse queries.

### Changes

- `start_to_close_timeout`: 60min -> 120min
- `maximum_attempts`: 2 -> 1 (retries not needed, runs are already scheduled and take longer than the schedule - FYI if the activity takes >15min, subsequent scheduled runs will be skipped - Temporal's default `SKIP`
author: pawel-cebula
summary: Increases the timeout for the quota limiting workflow to prevent failures due to long-running queries.

No

title: fix: run label filter on timing guard drop
body:
## Problem

PR #46053 added `team_id` labels to DB query timing histograms (`flags_person_query_time`, `flags_cohort_query_time`, `flags_group_query_time`, `flags_definition_query_time`).
These metrics are recorded via `TimingGuard`, which writes directly to Prometheus without applying the global `team_id_label_filter`.

This means every unique team ID becomes a distinct label value (~6k teams), instead of collapsing untracked teams to `"unknown"` (~55 values).

## Fix

Apply `apply_lab
author: matheus-vb
summary: Fixes the application of team ID labels in DB query timing histograms to ensure accurate metrics reporting.

title: fix(experiments): Filter precomputed exposures by exp dates

Added a test

title: fix(llma): refactor generation-level clustering to use subqueries
body:
## Problem

Generation-level clustering returns 0 items for teams despite matching embeddings existing in ClickHouse. The root cause is the data pipeline materializing ~117K generation UUIDs into Python and building a massive `IN ('uuid1', ..., 'uuid117550')` clause (~4.7 MB), which silently fails. When the same logic is expressed as a ClickHouse subquery (`IN (SELECT ...)`), it works correctly.

## Changes

**`data.py`** - Replace 3-hop Python ID materialization with inline ClickHouse subquerie
author: andrewm4894
summary: Fixed a critical issue in generation-level clustering that caused it to return zero items for teams due to inefficient data handling.

title: chore: add .watchmanconfig to reduce recrawl warnings
body:
Django's autoreload (used by celery worker/beat in dev) picks watchman when available, creating ~2000 subscriptions on the repo root. Without an ignore list, watchman indexes everything — node_modules, rust/target, .git, .flox, etc. — causing frequent recrawls (73 observed in a single session).

Adds a `.watchmanconfig` ignoring heavy dirs that no subscriber cares about. Only active watchman consumers are celery beat + worker, which only watch `.py` files in `posthog/`, `ee/`, `products/`.
author: webjunkie
summary: Added a configuration file to optimize the development environment by reducing unnecessary file indexing.

title: fix(devenv): create local symlink when resolving worktree config
body:
`dev:generate` falls through to the main repo config when run from a worktree without a local config. But `bin/start` only checks the local `$REPOSITORY_ROOT` path, so it never finds the generated config and silently falls back to the raw `bin/mprocs.yaml` with all processes enabled and no intent filtering.

The wizard (`dev:setup`) already handles this by creating a symlink from the worktree to the main repo config — `dev:generate` now does the same in `get_generated_mprocs_path()`.
author: webjunkie
summary: Fixes the local symlink creation for worktree configurations in the development environment.

title: chore(ux/search): add include_counts optimization to skip unnecessary COUNT queries

title: fix(mcp): return plaintext client_secret in DCR response
body:
## Problem

Follow-up to #48397. Django OAuth Toolkit hashes `client_secret` on save via `ClientSecretField.pre_save()`. Reading `app.client_secret` after save returns the pbkdf2 hash (e.g. `pbkdf2_sha256$600000$...`), not the plaintext.

This caused `invalid_client` errors during token exchange because Claude would store the hash as its "secret", send it in the token request, and django-oauth-toolkit would hash it *again* — so it never matched.

## Changes

- Generate the plaintext secret befor
author: MattBro
summary: Fixes the client_secret handling in the DCR response to prevent invalid_client errors during token exchange.

title: fix(session-recordings): use LEFT JOIN instead of IN/NOT IN for cohort filtering
body:
## Summary

Fixes OOM errors when using "filter out internal users" with cohort filters in session recordings for large customers.

Related ticket: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/50643

**The Problem:**
When using "NOT IN cohort" for filtering (like in "filter out internal users"), the query generated `notIn(person_id, (SELECT ... FROM cohortpeople))`. This caused ClickHouse to load ALL person_ids from the cohort subquery into RAM, causing OOM errors for large customers.
author: dmarticus
summary: Fixed out-of-memory errors in session recordings for large customers by optimizing cohort filtering.

title: fix(mcp): support confidential clients in OAuth DCR endpoint
body:
## Problem

Claude Desktop and claude.ai register as **confidential clients** (`token_endpoint_auth_method: "client_secret_post"`) during Dynamic Client Registration (DCR). Our DCR endpoint only accepted **public clients** (`"none"`), causing registration to fail with a 400 error and breaking the entire OAuth flow for these MCP clients.

We discovered this by setting up a local MCP server + PostHog backend with tunnels (cloudflared + ngrok) and inspecting the actual DCR request that Claude Deskt
author: MattBro
summary: Fixed the OAuth DCR endpoint to support confidential clients, resolving critical registration issues.

title: fix(insights): cache incomplete segment styling in line graph
body:
## Problem

Rendering area graph insights was very slow, due to per-segment work in Chart.js scriptable callbacks, especially creating a new pinstripe pattern for each segment render.

## Changes

- Compute incomplete-series flags and cutoff index once per dataset.
- Create the pinstripe pattern once per dataset (instead of in every segment callback).
- Only register `segment.borderDash` / `segment.backgroundColor` callbacks when incomplete styling is needed, so Chart.js skips callback overhead
author: thmsobrmlr
summary: Improved the performance of rendering area graph insights by optimizing segment styling caching.

title: refactor: Use functional state updates for state setters
body:
## Problem

The current state management in several components is using direct state updates, which can lead to race conditions when the new state depends on the previous state. This pattern is not recommended in React as it may not correctly capture the latest state value.

## Changes

Updated state update functions in multiple components to use the functional update pattern (`setState(prev => ...)`) instead of directly referencing the current state value. This ensures that state updates are ba
author: rafaeelaudibert
summary: Updated state management in components to prevent potential race conditions by using functional state updates.

title: fix: Add title attributes to iframe elements
body:
## Problem

Accessibility is a key aspect of web development, and our application currently lacks proper title attributes for iframe elements. This is important for screen reader users to understand the content of iframes, and it's a requirement for WCAG compliance.

## Changes

Added title attributes to all iframe elements across the application to improve accessibility. This includes:
- Heatmap exports and browsers
- Toolbar browser previews
- Shared insight previews
- Embedded queries
- Email
author: rafaeelaudibert
summary: Enhanced accessibility by adding title attributes to iframe elements across the application for better screen reader support.

title: perf: Use lazy initialization for useState calls
body:
## Problem

The current implementation of useState hooks in various components is not following the recommended pattern for expensive initial state calculations. When a function is passed directly to useState without wrapping it in a function, it gets executed on every render rather than just once during initialization.

## Changes

Updated useState hooks across multiple components to use the function initialization pattern `useState(() => ...)` instead of `useState(...)` when the initial state
author: rafaeelaudibert
summary: Improved performance by implementing lazy initialization for useState hooks across multiple components.

title: perf: Add passive event listeners to scroll and touch events
body:
## Problem

The application is experiencing performance issues with scroll and touch events. These events are not marked as passive, which can cause unnecessary browser repainting and lead to jank during scrolling, especially on mobile devices.

## Changes

Added the `{ passive: true }` option to event listeners for:
- `touchmove` in ResizeElement component
- `scroll` in VisibilitySensor component (also fixed a memory leak by properly defining the handler function)
- `scroll` in useScrollObserve
author: rafaeelaudibert
summary: Enhanced application performance by adding passive event listeners to scroll and touch events to reduce jank on mobile devices.

title: refactor: Extract component functions to top-level for better reusability
body:
## Problem

This PR refactors several components to improve code organization by moving nested component definitions outside of their parent components. This pattern helps with code readability and maintainability by avoiding deeply nested component definitions.

## Changes

- Moved `SupportFormBlock` component outside of `SidePanelSupport` and updated its props
- Moved `ProjectDragMonitor` component outside of `useProjectDragState` and made it accept props
- Moved `GoalTooltip` component outsid
author: rafaeelaudibert
summary: Improved code organization and maintainability by extracting component functions to top-level definitions.

title: feat(mcp): add client_name to analytics events for DCR client grouping
body:
## Problem

With Dynamic Client Registration (DCR), each MCP client installation (e.g. each Claude Code instance) registers its own anonymous OAuth client. This means the same application creates many different `client_id`s, making it impossible to aggregate MCP usage by application name (e.g. "how many tool calls came from Claude Code vs Cursor?").

The OAuth `client_name` (the human-readable application name) is available on the `OAuthApplication` model but was not being returned by the in
author: MattBro
summary: This PR adds the client_name to analytics events, enabling better aggregation of usage data for different MCP clients.

title: chore(flags): validate operator when creating new flag
body:
The feature flag serializer wasn't validating that property operators are valid values. An invalid operator like "`contains`" (instead of "`icontains`") could be saved, which then causes flags to fail evaluation at runtime.

Added a check in `validate_filters` that rejects unknown operators before persisting. The valid set is derived from the `OperatorType` so it stays in sync.

Added a test too.

summary: Enhanced validation for feature flag operators to prevent runtime evaluation failures.

title: fix(phai): Replace exception-based control flow with return value in memory parser
body:
## Problem

The memory collection completion logic uses exception-based control flow, so they're propagated in LLMA. This PR refactors it to use a check.

## Changes

- Renamed `raise_memory_updated` to `check_memory_collection_completed` to better reflect its new behavior
- Changed `check_memory_collection_completed` to return `None` instead of raising `MemoryCollectionCompleted` when collection is complete
- Updated `MemoryCollectorNode.arun()` to check for `None` return value instead
author: skoob13
summary: Refactored memory parser to improve control flow by using return values instead of exceptions.

title: feat(e2e): switch chromium to new headless mode

Also cleans up the old commented-out `--headless=new` flags block, which this replaces entirely.

Stacked on the Playwright 1.58.2 upgrade. Worth watching CI to see if any visual snapshot diffs emerge from the rendering change

summary: Enabled new headless mode for Chromium in end-to-end tests, enhancing rendering accuracy.

title: fix(experiments): gracefully handle StatisticError instead of propagating to logs

## Problem

When experiments have very little data, the stats library throws `StatisticError` exceptions during statistical evaluation (e.g. zero variance, near-zero control mean, invalid `sum_squares` constraints). These bypass the existing `validate_variant_result()` checks and get caught by `@experiment_error_handler`, which:

1. Logs them at `ERROR` level with full stack traces
2. Captures them to Posthog via `capture_exception()`
3. Converts them to a `ValidationError` that kills the
author: andehen

title: feat(ci): publish playwright report to cloudflare pages on PRs
body:
Deploys the Playwright HTML report to Cloudflare Pages after each E2E run so reviewers get a direct link without having to download artifacts from GitHub.

**How it works**

- After tests finish, wrangler deploys `playwright/playwright-report/` to the `playwright-report` CF Pages project
- Each PR gets a stable URL via `--branch=pr-{number}` — reruns overwrite, no accumulation
- A single PR comment is upserted (find-or-update) with pass/fail status and the report link
- Only runs on non-fork PRs
author: webjunkie
summary: Implemented publishing of Playwright reports to Cloudflare Pages for easier access to test results during PR reviews.

title: fix(llma): guard against negative input costs when cache tokens exceed input tokens
body:
## Problem

Customer reported all their LLM events have negative `$ai_input_cost_usd` values, breaking cost calculations. Root cause: #48021 introduced inclusive token accounting for Vercel gateway Anthropic events, but `posthog-ai` SDK reports exclusive (Anthropic-style) token counts even through the Vercel gateway. This causes `uncachedTokens` to go negative (e.g., `247 - 6287 = -6040`), producing negative costs.

## Changes

Adds a mathematical guard in the Anthropic cost calculation pa
author: andrewm4894
summary: Fixed an issue causing negative input cost calculations for LLM events, ensuring accurate cost reporting.

title: feat(mcp): improve truncation in MCP
body:
## Problem

Query result formatting currently always truncates long cell values (>500 characters) in SQL results. This behavior should be configurable to allow tools like the SQL MCP tool to return full, untruncated results when needed.

## Changes

Added a `truncate_results` parameter (default `True`) throughout the query execution and formatting pipeline:

1. **Query Executor** (`query_executor.py`):
   - Added `truncate_results` parameter to `arun_and_format_query()` and `_compress_results()`
author: skoob13
summary: Introduced a configurable truncation option for SQL query results, allowing users to retrieve full data when needed.

title: chore: use PascalCase for generated Orval enum keys
body:
Stacked on #47946

**Context**
Orval generates const objects for OpenAPI enums with keys matching the Python enum values verbatim (snake_case). In TS, PascalCase is the idiomatic convention for enum members.

**Change**
Adds `namingConvention: { enum: 'PascalCase' }` to the Orval config so generated enums look like:

```ts
{ IngestFirstEvent: 'ingest_first_event' }
```

instead of:

```ts
{ ingest_first_event: 'ingest_first_event' }
```

Nobody consumes generated enum members yet (confirmed zero
author: webjunkie
summary: Updated enum key naming convention for generated Orval enums to PascalCase for consistency.

…ters

Previously the annotation content field was limited to 400 characters, which was too restrictive for users wanting to add longer annotations with links (e.g., links to app builds for QA/testing).

Increasing the length in postgres is [a safe operation](https://oneuptime.com/blog/post/2026-02-02-postgresql-database-migrations/view#:~:text=Changing%20column%20types%20often%20requires,TYPE%20VARCHAR(100);%20CO

title: fix(oauth): allow self-introspection of expired tokens per RFC 7662
body:
## Problem

Users of the PostHog MCP server get **500 errors instead of 401** when their OAuth access tokens expire ([Slack thread](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C0AEPTEPTFC/p1771134233679559)).

**Root cause:** The introspection endpoint's `verify_request` method checks `token.is_valid()` for self-introspection. When a token is expired, this returns `False`, so the request is rejected with 403 instead of returning `{"active": false}`. This means the MCP server's `StateManager._fetchApiKey(
author: MattBro

title: fix(persons): person deletion bug during distinct id reset
body:
## Problem

When a person is deleted and their distinct ID is later reused,
the reset endpoint (`POST /api/projects/{id}/persons/reset_person_distinct_id`) only fixes the `person_distinct_id2` table in ClickHouse.
The `person` table is left with `is_deleted=1` at a high version, making the person invisible to all analytics queries.

Here's the full sequence that triggers the bug:

1. **Delete person** — Postgres hard-deletes the records. ClickHouse soft-deletes with `is_deleted=1, versio

title: feat: PersonHog-Coordination PoC
body:
## Problem

PersonHog processes person data sharded across Kafka partitions. Each partition must be owned by exactly one writer pod at a time. When pods scale up, scale down, or crash, partitions need to move between pods without losing inflight requests. We need a coordination layer that handles dynamic partition assignment and orchestrates zero-downtime handoffs.

## Changes

New `personhog-coordination` crate providing etcd-based coordination primitives:

- **Core types** — `Registere

title: fix: stop Temporal workflow interceptor from double-capturing activity errors
body:
## Problem

The Temporal `PostHogClientInterceptor` captures every activity failure twice: once at the activity level (correct) and once at the workflow level (broken). The workflow-level capture wraps the original exception in `ApplicationError` and loses the Python traceback during Temporal serialization. Without stack frames, cymbal includes the error message in the fingerprint — so any variable data in the message (token counts, request IDs) creates a unique issue per occurrence.

In 2 d
author: carlos-marchal-ph
summary: Fixed an issue where the Temporal workflow interceptor was double-capturing activity errors, preserving the original exception's traceback.

title: fix(cohorts): prevent replica sync blocking on static cohort deletion
body:
## Problem

When deleting a person from a static cohort, ClickHouse throws a `CHQueryErrorUnfinished` error with the message:

```
DB::Exception: Mutation is not finished because some replicas are inactive right now: e-02. 
Mutation will be done asynchronously.
```

This happens when some ClickHouse replicas are inactive and cannot confirm the DELETE mutation, even though the function was already using `mutations_sync=0`.

This is a follow up to the PR https://github.com/PostHog/posth
author: gustavohstrassburger
summary: Fixes an issue where deleting a person from a static cohort could block replica sync due to inactive ClickHouse replicas.

title: fix(ph-ai): fixed properties query sr filters
body:
## Problem

Session Recording metrics like `click_count`, `keypress_count` and others, are aggregate columns in the session recordings query, thjey should only exist in the `HAVING` clause.

When ph-ai put `keypress_count` with type: "recording" in the filter_group, we had a situation in which the query runner 

```
Couldn't strip it --> UnexpectedQueryProperties --> tried to apply WHERE clause --> wrong!
```

A filter like `keypress_count` >= xyz becomes a `HAVING`.

This should fix

title: chore(ds): improve pipeline v3 producer reliability and performance
body:
## Problem

Pipeline v3 could silently lose Kafka messages during flush — errors were logged as warnings but execution continued, potentially causing data inconsistency. Additionally, the S3 writer was creating a new S3 client per write and using less efficient compression.

## Changes

  * Added configurable `acks` and `enable_idempotence` parameters to `_KafkaProducer`
  * Changed WarpStream Kafka producer to use `acks=all` and `enable_idempotence=True` for stronger delivery guarantees
author: estefaniarabadan
summary: Improved the reliability and performance of the pipeline v3 producer to prevent data loss and enhance efficiency.

title: feat(signals): Improve cluster weight calculation based on team-relative impact
body:
## Problem

The current signal weighting system for video segment clusters uses occurrence count for weighting, mostly. It's pretty naive, but one thing about it is it doesn't account for team size, resulting in the same weight for an signal that affect 10 users, whether a team has 20 or 2000 active users.

## Changes

Adding a team-relative impact weighting system that calculates signal importance based on the ratio of affected users to total active users. Improved the weight range from 0.1-0.5
author: Twixes
summary: Introduces a team-relative impact weighting system for video segment clusters to improve signal importance calculation.

title: fix(feature-flags): use bulk operations in bulk_delete endpoint
body:
## Summary

The bulk_delete endpoint was slow because it called `flag.save()` for each flag, triggering 4 cache invalidation signals per flag. For e.g. 690 flags, this meant 2,760 Celery tasks all doing the same thing (rebuilding the cache for the same team).

**Before:** N saves → 4N signal handlers → 4N Celery tasks
**After:** 1-2 bulk updates → 0 signal handlers → 4 Celery tasks

### Changes
- Use `queryset.update()` for batch DB writes instead of individual `flag.save()` calls
- Use
author: dmarticus
summary: Improves the performance of the bulk_delete endpoint by optimizing database operations, reducing unnecessary tasks.

title: fix(geoip): handle missing MMDB file gracefully instead of crashing
body:
## Problem

`createHub()` unconditionally creates a `GeoIPService` and calls `await geoipService.get()`, which loads the MMDB file from disk. If the file doesn't exist, `loadMmdb()` throws an unhandled exception that crashes the process.

This is a problem for services like `recording-api` that:
- Run on the `posthog-node` Docker image, which does **not** include the GeoLite2 MMDB file (unlike the Python `posthog` image which downloads it during build)
- Don't have the S3 assets volume mou
author: TueHaulund
summary: Implements graceful handling of missing MMDB files to prevent crashes in the geo IP service.

title: fix: modal replay hangs due to init fail
body:
## Problem

When opening a recording in the modal (from experiments for example), the player hangs at "Buffering..." forever. The `rrweb` replayer needs both the DOM container and snapshot data to initialize, but on prod the DOM is ready before data arrives from the network. Both existing call sites for `tryInitReplayer` fire too early (before snapshots are loaded)and bail out. When data finally arrives, initialization is not fetched so the replayer is never created. Clicking on a segment in t

summary: Fixes an issue where the modal replay hangs indefinitely due to initialization failure, improving the reliability of session recordings.

title: fix(heatmap): Refactor heatmap data update logic
body:
## Problem

When scrolling with the heatmap toolbar open, canvas elements accumulate — each scroll tick creates a new overlapping `<canvas>`, making the heatmap increasingly opaque and unusable.

**Root cause**: PR #45974 changed `setHeatmapContainer`'s dependencies from `[]` to `[updateHeatmapData, heatmapJSColorGradient]`. Since `updateHeatmapData` depends on `heatmapJsData`, which depends on `heatmapScrollY` (updated every 50ms), the ref callback changed constantly. React re-invokes ref c
author: jabahamondes
summary: Fixed an issue where overlapping canvas elements made the heatmap unusable during scrolling.

title: fix(hogql): preserve cte declaration order in cpp parser backend
body:
## Problem

The C++ HogQL parser (cpp-json backend) loses CTE declaration order because it serializes the ctes dict as a JSON object keyed by name, and the underlying std::map sorts keys alphabetically. 

## Changes

Update cpp-json backend to preserve CTE declaration order.

## How did you test this code?

Unit tests

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: georgemunyoro
summary: Fixes the C++ HogQL parser to preserve the order of CTE declarations, improving query handling.

title: fix(taxonomic-filter): prevent scrollToRow on empty list
body:
## Problem

The `useEffect` calling `scrollToRow` in `InfiniteList` only checked `index >= 0` but not whether the list actually had any rows. When a remote data source returned 0 results with no search query, the `List` rendered with `rowCount=0` while `index` defaulted to `0`, causing `react-window` to throw a `RangeError`.

This happens because the `showEmptyState` guard intentionally allows the `<List>` to render with 0 rows when `hasRemoteDataSource=true` and `searchQuery=''` (assuming w
author: christiaan-ph
summary: Prevents a RangeError in the TaxonomicFilter component when rendering an empty list.

title: fix(phai): replace DataTableNode with DataVisualizationNode
body:
## Problem

The codebase was using `DataTableNode` in several places. That schema doesn't support pagination and loads rows in full, so it leads to poor performance.

## Changes

- Removed `DataTableNode` imports from multiple files
- Replaced all `DataTableNode` type references with `DataVisualizationNode`
- Updated the utility function `isDataTableNode` to `isDataVisualizationNode`
- Changed `NodeKind.DataTableNode` to `NodeKind.DataVisualizationNode` in query construction logic

Fi
author: skoob13
summary: Replaced the inefficient DataTableNode with DataVisualizationNode to improve performance.

title: fix(ingestion): correct vercel gateway anthropic cache accounting
body:
## Problem

AI generation costs are overcounted for Anthropic models routed through Vercel AI Gateway. Those events report inclusive input token counts, but our Anthropic input-cost path assumed input tokens excluded cache tokens and added cache costs on top, double-counting cache usage.

## Changes

- Updated Anthropic input-cost handling in `nodejs/src/ingestion/ai/costs/input-costs.ts`.
- Added an inclusive-token mode for Vercel gateway events (`$ai_provider=gateway` and `$ai_framework
author: richardsolomou
summary: Corrected the accounting for AI generation costs in the Vercel gateway to prevent double-counting cache usage.

title: feat(rust): new Rust app lifecycle management library to replace common/health
body:
## Problem
The current `common/health` library has some friction points I'd like to address:
* Requires reporting health status back to the registry for all components
* Doesn't support graceful shutdown
* Doesn't integrate k8s preStop -> terminationGraceSeconds handling
* Doesn't handle thread starvation or utilization-related skew/delays when app resources are saturated
* Doesn't integrate rich shutdown reporting
    * Which component triggered shutdown (or k8s/deploy?)
    * Which com
author: eli-r-ph
summary: Introduces a new Rust app lifecycle management library to enhance component health reporting and graceful shutdown capabilities.

title: fix(session-replay): work around Chrome GPU compositing bug causing replay to overlap UI
body:
## Summary

- Caps the replay player scale factor at `0.999` instead of `1.0` to work around a Chrome GPU compositor bug
- When `transform: scale(1)` (an identity transform) is applied to the `.replayer-wrapper`, Chrome's compositor can optimize it away, which breaks clipping from `overflow: hidden` on ancestor elements — causing the iframe's composited layer to paint outside its bounds and overlap the PostHog UI
- A non-identity transform like `scale(0.999)` forces Chrome to run the full compos
author: TueHaulund
summary: Addresses a Chrome GPU compositing bug that caused session replays to overlap with the PostHog UI.

This prop triggers the `submitAiRecommendation()` function when Enter is pressed and the input field contains text. The `LemonTextArea` component handles this by allowing

title: feat(session-recording): snapshot store loading pipeline
body:
## Summary

Adds a new `SnapshotStore` + `LoadingScheduler` based loading pipeline for session recordings, behind the `REPLAY_SNAPSHOT_STORE` feature flag. This replaces the legacy `REPLAY_TIMESTAMP_BASED_LOADING` flag and loading approach.

### Architecture

- **`SnapshotStore`** — manages source entries and loaded snapshot data. Tracks which sources are loaded, provides `canPlayAt()` for playability checks, and exposes a `version` counter for bridging mutable state with kea's reactive selector
author: TueHaulund
summary: Introduced a new loading pipeline for session recordings to enhance performance.

title: feat(flags): limit flag counts and filter sizes per team
body:
## Problem

Teams with an extreme number of feature flags or very large filter configurations can cause memory issues during flag evaluation and caching. There are no guardrails to prevent a single team from consuming excessive resources.

## Changes

Adds two configurable limits to protect against extreme outlier teams:

- **Flag count limit** (`MAX_FEATURE_FLAGS_PER_TEAM`, default 2,000): prevents creating new flags when a team exceeds the limit. Only enforced on create, not update.
- **Per-fl
author: haacked
summary: Implemented limits on feature flag counts and filter sizes to prevent resource overconsumption by teams.

title: fix: remove outside clickable area in piecharts
body:
## Problem

Clicking anywhere in the rectangular bounding box of a pie chart (minus padding) triggers the session recording modal. Only clicking directly on a pie slice should be interactive.

## Changes

- Changed Chart.js `hover.mode` from '`index`' to '`point`' with `intersect: true` so hover effects only activate on actual slices
- Replaced shared `onChartHover`/`onChartClick` delegation with pie-specific handlers that use '`point`' intersection mode, ensuring clicks and pointer cursor only
author: fercgomes
summary: Fixed the pie chart interaction to ensure only clicks on actual slices trigger the session recording modal.

title: fix(llm-analytics): re-apply tab-aware changes with bug fix
body:
## Problem

PR #47655 made LLM analytics tab-aware, but introduced a bug where clicking different generations within a trace view didn't switch content — the URL updated but the content stayed on the first generation. This was reverted in #47917.

The root cause: `llmAnalyticsTraceDataLogic` and `llmAnalyticsSessionDataLogic` connected to their scene logics (`llmAnalyticsTraceLogic`, `llmAnalyticsSessionLogic`) without passing `tabId`. Since the scene logics are keyed by `tabId`, the data logics
author: richardsolomou
summary: This fix resolves a bug in LLM analytics where switching between generations in a trace view did not update the displayed content.

title: fix(llm-analytics): preserve filters when navigating back from detail views
body:
## Problem

When navigating between LLMA list/detail views (traces, sessions, evaluations, prompts, datasets), filter state and URL state could diverge. This showed up as:

- back navigation unexpectedly resetting to defaults
- refresh restoring a different state than what was visible before refresh
- detail breadcrumbs/back links not always preserving list filter context

## Changes

This PR now aligns LLMA behavior with the Error tracking URL model: URL is canonical state.

### 1. URL-to-state
author: richardsolomou
summary: This fix ensures that filter states are preserved when navigating back from detail views in LLM analytics.

body:
## Problem

The current development environment startup experience lacks a clear information display. Users don't have an easy way to see what's running or how to configure their environment.

Also we currently log some information (like news about the new `hogli dev:setup`) but we only see it once 
we close mprocs which isn't ideal

summary: Introduced an informative welcome screen for the development environment to enhance user awareness of the setup process.

title: feat(deduplicator): use spawn_blocking to get long-running IO bound work off default tokio worker pool
body:
## Problem
Logs indicate we are occasionally losing pods when the `_liveness` endpoint becomes unresponsive due to thread starvation on the main pool. Some long running tasks like RocksDB store flush can use 8 threads in parallel, doesn't yield, and were tracked taking 30-50 seconds per store which in parallel (we allow up to 6 partitions at once to export atm, and would like to do more) will run out the clock.
<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<
author: eli-r-ph
summary: Implemented a new method to handle long-running IO tasks to prevent thread starvation and improve system reliability.

whew
ran a bunch of diagnostic scripts figuring out exactly what query ended up being produced by our Session Replay filters, and saw that recording that DID NOT match the filters were being incorrectly filtered out

There are a few closely related clickhouse issues (allsince resolved) to indicate this might not be the most shocking thing: 


 - #56790: GLOBAL IN doesn't work correctly - https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/56790
 - #84718: ORDER BY + LIMIT corruption http

title: feat(analytics-platform): Support variable TTL for different ages of data
body:
## Problem

We might want to preaggregate data with a different TTL depending on how recent the data is. You might want a TTL of only 15 minutes for the current day, but data older than 30 days could have a TTL of weeks.

## Changes

You can now do something like this:
```typescript
preagg_result = ensure_preaggregated(
    team=self.team,
    insert_query="""SELECT whatever FROM STUFF""",
    time_range_start=datetime(2026, 02, 13),
    time_range_end=datetime(2025, 01, 13),
    #
author: robbie-c
summary: Introduces support for variable TTL for preaggregated data based on its age, enhancing data management flexibility.

title: fix(funnels): fix data warehouse id column with decimal type
body:
## Problem

Experienced an issue when trying to setup a data warehouse funnel for a customer.

We use PostHog SQL `toUUIDOrDefault()` (ClickHouse `accurateCastOrNull()`) to convert the id column, which might or might not be a valid UUID. When the column is not a UUID it's expected to fall back to the default - a generated UUID. Unfortunately ClickHouse behaviour is inconsistent here: If you pass in a string that can't be parsed into a valid UUID, it falls back to the default. However if you
author: thmsobrmlr
summary: Fixed an issue with the data warehouse funnel setup that affected customer data accuracy.
